---
title: "Week 14 — Meta-analysis and forest plots"
toc: true
---

> **Status:** placeholder. Real content will be developed during the
> course build.

## Big question

How do studies become evidence?

## Day-by-day

### Monday — concept

Single studies, systematic reviews, and meta-analysis; the parts of a
forest plot.

### Wednesday — micro-lab ticket

Annotate a forest plot: identify study rows, effect measure, pooled
estimate, and heterogeneity cues.

### Friday — quiz / application

Quiz mirroring Wednesday's micro-lab on a new forest plot;
meta-analysis critique application.

## Primary source basis

No IMS content. Custom-built from cited published forest plots and
the instructor's existing publication-bias / rigor work.

## Supplemental source / custom needs

Externally-sourced forest plots (with attribution); a one-page
meta-analysis mini-assignment worksheet.

## Learning goals

Students should be able to:

- [ ] Distinguish a systematic review from a meta-analysis.
- [ ] Identify the parts of a forest plot.
- [ ] Interpret a pooled estimate.
- [ ] Explain heterogeneity conceptually.
- [ ] Name reasons a meta-analysis can mislead.

## Assessment links

Links to the bank/manifests for this week will appear here once items
are built.

- Micro-lab ticket: TBD
- Friday quiz: TBD
- Homework: TBD
- Meta-analysis mini-assignment: TBD
